question:
Will a team from LCK (South Korea) win LoL Worlds 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the region or country of the esports team winning 1st place in the League of Legends 2025 Season World Championship (Worlds 2025).

If the winner of Worlds 2025 is not determined by December 31, 2025 ET, this market will resolve to Other.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Liquipedia (https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page).

Teams counted toward each region:
- LCK (South Korea): Gen.G, T1, KT Rolster, Hanwha Life Esports
- LPL (China): Bilibili Gaming, Top Esports, Invictus Gaming, Anyone’s Legend
- LEC (Europe / EMEA): G2 Esports, Fnatic, Movistar KOI
- LTA (Americas): FlyQuest, 100 Thieves, Vivo Keyd Stars
- LCP (Pacific): PSG Talon, CTBC Flying Oyster, Team Secret Whales

evidences.title: All LoL Worlds Champions
evidences.content: South Korean teams (LCK) and Chinese teams (LPL) have dominated the championship in the past decade, with a lone European champion in the very first Worlds. ## Regional Dominance and Repeat Champions South Korea’s LCK is the most dominant region in Worlds history. Korean teams have won a total of 8 World Championships as of 2024. These title runs include a famous dynasty by SK Telecom T1 (now T1) and additional championships by Samsung’s teams and DAMWON Gaming. [...] Q: Who won LoL Worlds each year? A: The world champions for every year are listed above in our complete winners table. In brief: Fnatic won the inaugural World Championship in 2011; Taipei Assassins won in 2012; SK Telecom T1 (T1) dominated 2013, 2015, 2016; Samsung White took 2014; Samsung Galaxy won 2017; Invictus Gaming and FunPlus Phoenix brought home 2018 and 2019 respectively for China; DAMWON Gaming won 2020; Edward Gaming won 2021; DRX won 2022; and T1 were champions in 2023 and 2024. Q: Which team has the most LoL Worlds titles? A: South Korea’s T1 (formerly SK Telecom T1) holds the record with five World Championship titles. Q: Which region has won the most World Championships? A: Korea (LCK) is the leadi...

evidences.title: League of Legends
evidences.content: LEC (Europe) Starting in 2023, LEC has the Spring split separated into Winter and Spring splits, making three splits in total. Starting in 2023, the teams invited to Worlds are determined in the LEC Season Finals, in which the top 6 teams from the three LEC splits compete. ### 2025-2026 [edit] Competitive teams are split into regions 2025: teams are split into 5 regions (Korea: LCK, China: LPL, Europe: LEC, Americas: LTA (separated into North and South Conferences), and Asia-Pacific: LCP). 2026: Americas are split into North America (LCS) and South America (CBLOL), forming 6 regions in total. [...] A year is separated into three splits, with the regional championships following the third split. The first split is designated as its own tournament, with the top team from each region qualifying for the First Stand Tournament (FST). The top 2 teams from each of the regions in the second split are invited to the Mid-Season Invitational (MSI). Like in 2024, the winner of the MSI is automatically invited to Worlds, given that they make it to playoffs afterwards. The top teams from each regional championship are invited to Worlds. At least 3 teams per region will qualify for Worlds, wit...

evidences.title: The Five Most Legendary Worlds Finals in League of Legends History | Esports Charts
evidences.content: The matchup was also a clash of regions’ pride: LCK vs. LPL, the dominant Korean squad versus a rising Chinese challenger. Hype was through the roof, with millions tuning in to watch this showdown live. In fact, the 2024 final hit a new record peak viewership of 6.9 million concurrent viewers (outside China), the highest in esports history. Faker claims a record fifth World Championship title / Photo by Riot Games [...] With that, EDG completed the upset 3:2, claiming their first-ever World Championship. The victory was monumental: not only did EDG bring the trophy back to China, but they also denied Korea a repeat title and became the third LPL team in history to lift the Cup. Chinese fans erupted in celebration: videos circulated of university students watching in dorms and then pouring into the streets to cheer. Even state media noted that about one in 30 people on the planet (mainly via Chinese streams) witnessed EDG’s victory live. The 2021 final is remembered for its gritty comeback and what it represented: an LCK juggernaut being toppled by the unlikeliest of LPL heroes. ## DRX vs. T1 (2022) [...] ## T1 vs. Bilibili Gaming (2024) Taking place in London’s O2 Arena, the 202...

The LCK leads with an impressive 9 titles, while the LPL has won 3 times. Europe and the PCS have each secured 1 title. evidences.title: 2025 Mid-Season Invitational - Liquipedia League of Legends Wiki
evidences.content: Bracket Stage - July 1st - 12th + 8 teams play double elimination bracket. + All matches are Bo5 with Fearless Draft. + The winner of MSI qualifies for Worlds if they reach their regional playoffs in Split 3 and the 2nd place region gets a 4th seed for its domestic league at Worlds. Patch Information + All matches are played in Patch 25.13 ### MSI 2025 Draw Show [edit] Bracket Stage + Click here for detailed information about Bracket Stage Draw show [...] ## Format [edit] Qualification + The top two teams from each region (10 total) participate in MSI. + The 1st seed of each region and 2nd seed of LCK (the winning region of FST 2025) will get a bye into the Bracket Stage, while the other 4 teams will start from Play-In. Play-In - June 27th - 29th + 4 teams play in a GSL-Style Group + Teams are seeded based on the results from FST 2025 + Top 2 teams (the 2-0 and 2-1 teams) advance the Bracket Stage. + All matches are Bo5 with Fearless Draft.

evidences.title: League of Legends Worlds 2025 Preview: China hype is real
evidences.content: ● Hanwha Life Esports (LCK, Korea): Currently the world’s top team, Hanwha Life Esports has performed exceptionally well with superlative players like Zeus and Viper. With their teamwork and strategic ability, they are a team to watch out for. ● Team Liquid (LTA, Americas): With a few regional titles and good showings on the international stage, Team Liquid is a team to be taken seriously. Their jungler Umti may struggle, however, against top competition. ● TOP Esports (LPL, China): Although inconsistent at times, TOP Esports features one of the strongest junglers in Kanavi, which enables it to overcome top teams. [...] In 2025, Worlds will again be the unforgettable spectacle it has grown used to delivering, with China hosting the tournament and the Grand Finals in Chengdu. The introduction of an entirely new Swiss stage format, combined with the addition of 17 top teams from Korea, China, Europe, the Americas, and the Asia-Pacific region, guarantees a highly competitive tournament. The League of Legends Worlds 2025 is scheduled to take place between October and early November. evidences.title: How To Qualify For The 2025 World Championships - FloTrack
evidences.content: null Qualifying for the 2025 World Championships isn't as straight-forward as it seems. With World Championship Provisionals, Wanda Diamond League Byes, and World Ranking quota in the conversation - athletes and fans have to navigate a mucky landscape that can leave you scratching your head. Here's a guide to help breakdown the path to World Championship qualification. To start, we'll discuss the US Track and Field Championships. The US Track and Field Championships serve as USATF's selection procedure. Other countries choose different ways of selecting their teams - for example, Great Britain automatically selects the top two in each event at its championship and leaves room for a discretionary third pick. Team USA takes discretion (for the most part) out of the conversation. [...] The top three finishers at the US Championships earn a Team USA Bid to the World Championship meet - as long as they have the World Championship Ranking Quota or a World Championship Standard. A World Championship Standard is a mark that athletes must achieve to be eligible to compete at a World Championship. If they do not have this standard, they must rely on the World Ranking Quota. World Rankings...

And that concludes all of the current winners from every League of Legends World Championship. From the first Summoner Cup winner, Fanatic all the way to T1's most recent win claiming their fifth Summoner Cup. But the question is now, who will be next? Will the LPL continue their rivalry with the LCK, or will another region rise up and steal the spotlight? And the big question every fan asks year after year, will North America ever win the Summoner Cup? Only time will tell, but [...] that will change today. The Crownless are finally king and Invictus Gaming are your 2018 World Champions. The season 9 World Championship finals took place in 2019 in Paris, France, featuring a total of 24 different teams from around the world. evidences.title: ALL WORLDS WINNING MOMENT! (2011-2024)

evidences.title: kt Rolster Stuns Gen.G, Hanwha Life Sweeps T1 in 2025 LCK Playoffs - Inven Global
evidences.content: # Inven Global League of Legends League of Legends # kt Rolster Stuns Gen.G, Hanwha Life Sweeps T1 in 2025 LCK Playoffs John Popko Twitter; "Tweet") Facebook; "Share") Reddit; "Submit") Pinterest; "Pin it") URL Underdogs kt Rolster and Hanwha Life Esports pulled off two of the biggest surprises of the League of Legends Champions Korea (LCK) season, advancing to the upper bracket finals of the 2025 playoffs and securing early berths at the World Championship. Both teams, written off by many analysts heading into the postseason, defied expectations. kt Rolster stunned regular-season leader Gen.G in a tense five-game series, while Hanwha Life Esports broke a four-match losing streak against T1 with a commanding sweep. ## 2025 LCK Playoffs Round 1 & 2 Results [...] Hanwha Life Esports delivered an equally shocking result the following day. Facing T1, a team they had not beaten in four consecutive meetings, Hanwha dominated in a 3-0 sweep. Kim “Zeka” Geon-woo’s Orianna and Park “Viper” Do-hyeon’s Lucian set the tone in Game 1 with explosive damage output. Despite T1’s attempts to claw back momentum in Games 2 and 3, Hanwha’s team coordination and late-game composure held strong. A de...

evidences.title: 2025 World Championship format overview
evidences.content: The 2025 World Championship uses a single-elimination knockout stage with best-of-five matches and Fearless Draft. Side selection rules depend on match record in quarterfinals, with coin flip used later. The tournament is played on Patch 25.20. Qualification includes the MSI champion if it reaches regional playoffs, top regional teams entering Swiss Stage, and additional Play-In spots tied to MSI regional performance.
